medical_history.chief_complaint: Acutely swollen tongue, palate, and submandibular region, one to two hours after waking
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented with acute angioedema, treated with 100 mg hydrocortisone and 4 mg Piriton, recovered well by the next morning
medical_history.past_medical_history: Right leg below knee amputation due to trauma, benign prostatic hypertrophy, peripheral vascular disease, non-insulin dependant diabetes
medical_history.family_history: No previous personal or family history of angioedema noted

physical_examination.general_examination: Alert, could breathe and swallow but was unable to talk, swelling subsided by the next morning
physical_examination.specialty_examination: No splenomegaly or lymphadenopathy noted during clinical examination by the haematologists

laboratory_examinations.findings: Slight increases in urea (8.8 mmol/litre; reference range, 3.3–6.7) and creatinine (130 mmol/litre; reference range, 60–120). C reactive protein was increased at 74 mg/litre (reference range, <5). All other routine biochemistry was normal and he was normocalcaemic.

C4 concentration was towards the bottom end of the reference range at 0.17 g/litre (reference range, 0.14–0.54), C3 was slightly high at 1.85 g/litre (reference range, 0.7–1.60), and C1 esterase inhibitor was low at 0.09 g/litre (reference range, 0.15–0.35). Total IgE was 16 kU/litre (reference range, <81).
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Repeat blood samples showed complete recovery of the complement profile (C3, 1.34 g/litre; C4, 0.17 g/litre; and C1 esterase inhibitor, 0.44 g/litre).

laboratory_examinations.findings: Slightly suppressed IgA (0.77 g/litre; reference range, 0.8–4.0) and IgM (0.31 g/litre; reference range, 0.5–2.0) and a monoclonal IgG k paraprotein band of 9.8 g/litre. The electrophoresis strip showed a slightly low polyclonal c region, indicating a low polyclonal IgG concentration. The serum B2 microglobulin concentration was estimated at 3.5 g/litre (reference range, <2.4). Total urine protein was 0.41 g/litre but there was no Bence Jones proteinuria present. The erythrocyte sedimentation rate was raised at 97 mm/hour (reference range, <10).
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: The IgG paraprotein was still present at 10.0 g/litre in repeat samples.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Original sample retested showed C1 esterase inhibitor at 0.49 g/litre (higher than normal and in keeping with an acute phase response). Reanalysis on Behring BNII nephelometer showed C3, 1.85 g/litre; C4, 0.17 g/litre; and C1 esterase inhibitor, 0.42 g/litre.

other_tests.findings: No evidence of increased plasma cell numbers, no collections of lymphocytes, and the Congo Red stain was normal—no evidence of myeloma, lymphoma, or amyloid present.

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Idiopathic angioedema with a monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined importance
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with acute angioedema and initially had a low C1 esterase inhibitor concentration, suggesting acquired angioedema possibly secondary to a B cell neoplasm. Further investigations revealed a monoclonal IgG k paraprotein, but bone marrow studies showed no evidence of myeloma, lymphoma, or amyloid. Repeat testing showed normal C1 esterase inhibitor levels, ruling out C1 esterase inhibitor deficiency. The final diagnosis was based on the absence of evidence for other conditions and the presence of a stable paraprotein.
